description: This stacked bar chart displays the quantitative oxide composition of passivation films formed on Fe-30Mn-8Al-C-xCu low-density steels, specifically comparing samples with 0 wt% Cu (0Cu) and 3 wt% Cu (3Cu). The data, calculated from XPS measurements according to Equation (5), represent stable passivation films obtained after potentiostatic polarization at -0.3 V (vs. Ag-AgCl) in 3.5 wt% NaCl solution for 7200 s. For the 0Cu sample, the passive film consists predominantly of Mn oxides (MnO 35.5%, MnO2 30%), Al2O3 (10.9%), and Fe oxides (Fe3O4 4.8%, Fe2O3 7.4%, FeOOH 5.9%), with minor metallic Al (5.5%). The 3Cu sample exhibits a markedly different composition: increased MnO (43.5%) but decreased MnO2 (24.6%) and Al2O3 (8.1%), alongside reduced Fe oxides (Fe3O4 4.3%, Fe2O3 6.7%, FeOOH 5.4%). Critically, the 3Cu film contains 1.1% Cu2O and 1% CuO, which are absent in 0Cu. The figure demonstrates that Cu alloying reduces the proportion of protective Fe and Al oxides while introducing unstable Cu oxides, correlating with enhanced microgalvanic corrosion activity in the high-Cu steel.
